Airbnb CEO Brian Chesky Says He's Been Keeping an Eye on RWA Tokenization for a While Now
> “Most of it is noise. But underneath the noise, something real is happening.”
> “The tokens & the blockchains aren't the interesting part”
> “The interesting part is what happens to friction.”

tweet.text:
I've been quietly keeping an eye on real-world asset tokenization for a while now.

Most of it is noise. But underneath the noise, something real is happening.

Here are a few thoughts.

A THREAD

tweet.text:
I think there is general confusion around how AI works, AI tokenomics, and ultimately *what is actually priced in* for the AI trade - and that some of the existing arguments are at odds with one another

Firstly to clear this up - what Brad and Gavin are saying are completely in agreement, what Gavin is laying out here is the *mega bull case* as he so states in the first sentence of his tweet lol

The base case we are all living with is that the labs are going to continue to generate a significant amount of revenue this year and next year. OpenAI was already the fastest growing company of all

sample_posts.text:
Human Archive Catalog
https://x.com/babugi28/status/2076822707187159085

tweet.text:
The Physical AI data space is exploding, but most vendors still share samples through Google Drive or Hugging Face links that are slow and painful to evaluate.

We built the Human Archive Catalog so researchers can quickly browse and filter our off-the-shelf datasets by environment, hardware configuration, modality, annotations, and more.

If you’re a robotics or world model researcher, request access below:

https://catalog.humanarchive-qa.ai/request-access

sample_posts.text:
data refineries: turning abundant data into trustworthy, actionable intelligence
this is the moat
https://x.com/michellelsun/status/2076705184898785637

tweet.text:
Data volume is no longer the bottleneck for robotics training. 

It’s building dynamic pipelines - like oil refineries in the AI age - that guarantee trustworthy lineage + usable signal for training and deployment.

A single robot spits out terabytes of raw camera, LiDAR & IMU streams daily. 

Training data supply is exploding globally, especially with the surge of data collection startups across India, China, and beyond.

In order for these data to be useful, we need robust processing pipelines that handle : 
- Provenance tracking (where did this frame come from? which embodiment? under what

> He proposes a U.S-led Frontier AI Standards Body to evaluate advanced models, set evolving safety benchmarks, audit frontier labs, and establ
https://research.auditless.com/p/should-you-tag-claude

sample_posts.text:
U.S.-Iran tensions amplify oil and inflation pressure –
Link
Escalating U.S.-Iran hostilities triggered reciprocal airstrikes and pushed Brent crude futures more than 3% higher to nearly $79 per barrel. Markets are watching for Strait of Hormuz disruptions that could lift energy prices, keep inflation sticky, and weigh on crypto risk assets.
Matters because:
Energy shocks can feed inflation and make Fed easing harder, which is typically negative for crypto.

convergence.context: 3 posts across 2 channels (muur_posts, cookiesreads) — mentioning U.S. Senate, Clarity Act, Donald Trump, Curve

sample_posts.text:
Clarity Act enters critical legislative window –
Link
The U.S. Senate's CLARITY Act entered a crucial four-week window that will decide whether it can pass before Congress adjourns in August. President Trump called for Senate passage, citing competition with China on crypto and AI.
Matters because:
The bill could improve regulatory certainty for U.S. digital asset markets if it advances in time.

tweet.text: Critical week for Clarity, which also happens to be the one-year anniversary of GENIUS. A reminder of the incredible amount of hard work that has gone into this bill, but also of the time we’ve already lost. We cannot afford to delay any longer.
